title: empetrum rubrum subsp. eamesii
intro: Empetrum rubrum subsp. eamesii, also known as Eames' crowberry, is a subspecies of the Empetrum rubrum plant, a low-growing, woody shrub native to the subalpine and alpine regions of the British Isles. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which are attributed to its high content of polyphenolic compounds and other bioactive substances. The health benefits of Eames' crowberry include its potential to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. In herbal medicine, Empetrum rubrum subsp. eamesii is often prepared as a tincture or infused in hot water to make a tea, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
